+++
title = "Local Groups Advocate for Expanded Community Energy Initiatives in Hastings and Rye"
date = "2026-03-05"
author = "Sarah Jenkins, Chief Reporter"
categories = ["Community"]
tags = ["Hastings","Rye","Energy","Renewable","Sustainability"]
draft = false
+++

In a recent gathering at the town hall, several local groups have come together to call on Hastings Borough Council and its counterpart in Rye for increased support and funding towards community energy projects. The meeting was attended by representatives from environmental organisations, local businesses, and residents who are all united under the shared goal of transitioning to renewable sources of power.

Participants emphasised the need to 'unlock community energy at scale'—a move that would see more community-owned wind turbines, solar panels, and battery storage projects being set up across both Hastings and Rye. Such initiatives not only promise economic benefits for local residents but also contribute significantly towards reducing carbon emissions and promoting sustainability in line with national climate targets.

During the meeting, there was a strong consensus among attendees that the success of such ventures hinges on adequate support from local authorities. This includes streamlined planning processes, financial incentives, and regulatory frameworks that encourage investment in clean energy solutions rather than hindering progress.

The event concluded with an open invitation to all community stakeholders to get involved or contribute their ideas towards achieving this ambitious yet necessary goal for the area's environmental future.

---
**Source:** [Original Article](https://news.google.)
